item.title: is this a copy???
item.content:encoded: this scp looks alot like bobble the clown from scp 1730

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i like the concept, but it doesn’t feel like much went on in this tbh, it just feels like exposition and not much else, no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotcha. I might recommend changing it to something like "a balloon needs to be provided for this task, as SCP-4671 cannot or will not manifest the balloon required."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In regards to the footnote, I mean he needs to be given a balloon. He can't manifest it for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dig this a lot; it strikes all the right notes of humorous/horrific/tragic to me. I do feel like Dr. Pierre's dialogue could use a little work; I understand that she's going out of her way to make Clarky feel at ease, but all the hemming and hawing and stifled laughter pulls me out of it. I hadn't seen your previous iterations of this, but FWIW I think it's great and hope you don't lean more into the comedy.

One thing, though, in your footnote - "…doesn't manifest the balloon necessary to perform said action unless provided." Maybe it's just me, but I can't follow this. Do you mean he needs to be provided the balloon, or be provoked to manifest the balloon?

item.title: Re: I love it!
item.content:encoded:
The only downside is the lack of a title in the main list, which I feel should be just something simple like "Clarky the Clown".

Actually, that is the title. I just did not add it because I normally do so if it is a success, but I guess there is no harm in doing so.

item.title: I love it!
item.content:encoded:
I really like how this one is, especially considering how clowns are considered evil in modern pop culture. It's great to have a clown that is simply misunderstood and wants to act like a normal clown. The only downside is the lack of a title in the main list, which I feel should be just something simple like "Clarky the Clown".

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually enjoy this one. The whole race-of-anomalous-killer-clowns thing works well in-universe, and like Dr. Cambrian and Even said before, the whole happiness thing could be an effect, and could also be the cause of the altercation mentioned by -4671 about the first group of humans it encountered. They could tell that something was off, and it freaked them out. Or hell, the effect could even be limited to certain criteria. Either way, I feel like this needs more upvotes and definitely shouldn't be in the negative. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's not much wrong with the scip itself, but imo it's hard to picture tribes of clowns when Herman Fuller's Circus lore already explains what clowns are and it's subdivisions.

(Pretty sure someone else said this one of the ecip's previous iterations)

item.title: Lots of potential!
item.content:encoded: I think overall it's good, and an entertaining read! As Doctor Cambrian mentioned, there are a few moments that seem a little off for Foundation personnel standards, but you could always wave that away as one of Clarky's anomalous effects if you'd prefer that to changing the tone. Other than that and some minor spelling errors it seems great! Best of luck in your continued efforts, this was a fun read. +1

Honestly? I really like it! there are a few moments that feel a bit close to breaching clinical tone, but the relationships between all the characters feel very realistic and well-defined. I think Clark really hits that fine line between being silly and still a believable and endearing character. Good job!

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Alright, I have done nearly every advice for this, and did the most common suggestions being expansion of the interview to other interviews, and touch more about Clarky's kind. Heck, I might just turn it into a comedic skip and remove the interviews all together (I feel like removing the last one) for maybe silly test logs or something.

I'm running out of ways to fix this from the input I have received for an earlier version and I'm not sure what is left to do. Feel free to toss me some advice if you like. I'm considering doing a collaboration with someone capable of salvaging what folks liked about this. Who knows. There are options.

Will post credits if this succeeds which I doubt.

P.S. By the way, sorry for practically being a broken record at this point.

Edit: Well, here is the credits. Thanks to the following folks for their critique.

  • Zyn
  • GentleGifts
  • Dr Guilford
  • RafiR
  • TheMightyMcB
  • DrDromeus
